name: Band Biceps Curl
resistance_profile: Elastic tension climbs as the band stretches, so it is hardest near full flexion — an ascending, peak-contraction-biased curl that fills the band-only gap.
cues: Keep the elbows pinned to your ribs
Fight the band on the way down instead of letting it snap back
Stand tall, no leaning back to help the lift
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a handle or the band in each hand with palms facing forward.
Pin your elbows to your sides and let the arms hang so there is light tension in the band at the bottom.
Curl your hands toward your shoulders, supinating slightly, until the band tension peaks near the top.
Squeeze the biceps hard at full flexion, then lower under control until the arms are fully extended and tension eases.
good_when: Training at home or while traveling with minimal equipment
You want strong tension in the peak-contracted position
High-rep pump or rehab work that is easy on the joints
bad_when: You need heavy, precisely quantifiable load to drive progression
You want maximal stretch tension at the bottom of the curl
common_errors: Letting the band recoil the arms down with no eccentric control
Standing on too little band so there is no tension at the bottom
progressions: Use a thicker band or choke up on it for more tension
Add a second band or widen your stance to increase resistance
regressions: Perform seated to remove body English
